pi_items.source: File verified to exist in env.json mount configuration; actual data file examined
Web search on eQUEST AZIMUTH conventions: 0°=North, 90°=East, 180°=South, 270°=West, 360°=North
Current MIN-SUPPLY-T in 4StoreyBuilding.inp is 55°F; target is 58°F. However, the parameter name in metadata/verifier (COOL-SET-T) does not match the actual eQUEST INP parameter name (MIN-SUPPLY-T)
Grep analysis of 4StoreyBuilding.inp found exactly 5 ground floor systems: G.S11, G.E12, G.N13, G.W14, G.C15
Found in FLOOR definitions (lines 516, 968, 1484, 1917): AZIMUTH = 360 (equivalent to 0° per eQUEST convention)
Parameter MIN-SUPPLY-T = 55 found in all 5 ground floor systems (G.S11, G.E12, G.N13, G.W14, G.C15) at lines 2602-2766
Grep for systems with zone names starting with G.: found exactly 5 systems named G.S11, G.E12, G.N13, G.W14, G.C15
Verifier looks for COOL-SET-T but the actual eQUEST INP parameter is MIN-SUPPLY-T (confirmed via web search of eQUEST documentation and actual INP file analysis)

privileged_info_summary: The 4StoreyBuilding.inp is a real calibrated building model (not synthetic) with 4 floors. Current building azimuth is 360 degrees (North); target is 90 degrees (East). The building has exactly 5 ground-floor packaged single-zone systems (G.S11, G.E12, G.N13, G.W14, G.C15). Each system currently has MIN-SUPPLY-T = 55°F; target is 58°F. CRITICAL: The metadata and verifier reference parameter name 'COOL-SET-T' which does not exist in eQUEST INP files; the actual parameter is 'MIN-SUPPLY-T'.
